summary: Matched the previously stubbed game-mode function fn_80182F40 in src/melee/gm/gm_17C0.c. The implementation drives a timed state sequence using lbl_804D65E8, gm_801A4BA8 frame/time checks, audio transitions, camera control, random camera angles, player CPU level changes, and scene-minor transition logic. Supporting header/type changes widened several narrow u8 return/prototype types to int and changed gm_8049E548_t.unk_8 from s8 to u8 to make call sites and code generation line up.
what_changed: Replaced the placeholder /// #fn_80182F40 with a full C implementation taking HSD_GObj* unused.
Added includes for <sysdolphin/baselib/random.h> and <melee/gm/gmmain_lib.h> in gm_17C0.c to support HSD_Randi and gmMainLib calls.
Implemented early exit handling when gm_801A36A0(4) has mask 0x1100 set: stops/updates audio, calls gm_801A4B60, gm_801A42E8(0), gm_801A42D4, then returns.

Implemented timeout logic for gm_801A4BA8() >= 0x4B0, including audio cleanup and conditional gmMainLib_8015DB18 plus gm_SetScenePendingMinor(0) when gm_801A42C4() == 3 and gmMainLib_8015DB00() is even.
Implemented lbl_804D65E8 state handling for cases 0, 1, 2, and an empty case 3.

In state 0 at time 0x190, selects gm_801BF6D8 as a camera/player focus, configures Camera_8002EEC8, Camera_8002E6FC, Camera_8002ED9C, Camera_8002EC7C, Camera_8002EB5C, lowers other player CPU levels to 2, and sets Player_SetMoreFlagsBit4 for all four players.
In state 1 at time 0x280 or when Player_800368F8(gm_801BF6D8()) is zero, selects gm_801BF6F8, configures the camera similarly, raises that player's CPU level to 9, and lowers the others to 2.
In state 2 at time 0x370 or when Player_800368F8(gm_801BF6F8()) is zero, calls Camera_8002F474 and restores all four players to CPU level 9 with Player_SetMoreFlagsBit4 cleared.
Changed Camera_8002E6FC parameter type from u8 to int in both camera.h and camera.dox.
Changed gm_801BF6D8 and gm_801BF6F8 return types from u8 to int in gm_1BA8.c and gm_1BA8.h.
Changed gm_8049E548_t.unk_8 in gm_1B03.h from s8 to u8.
smart_moves: The match was supported by small signature corrections rather than forcing casts at the new call sites; Camera_8002E6FC, gm_801BF6D8, and gm_801BF6F8 were widened to int where the generated code apparently needed int-valued arguments/results.
The implementation keeps the unused HSD_GObj* parameter named unused, documenting that the callback signature matters even though the body does not use the object.
The state machine mirrors the likely assembly structure directly: early returns first, then a switch on lbl_804D65E8 with explicit cases and frame equality checks.
Temporary locals temp_r31 and temp_r31_2 preserve selected player indices before passing them to Camera_8002E6FC, a common decomp tactic when register lifetime/order matters.
Random camera angle expressions use the explicit radian conversion constant 0.017453292F and HSD_Randi ranges seen in the diff, avoiding over-abstraction that could disturb matching.
decomp_lessons: When a matched function calls helpers returning small integer fields, the correct C prototype may need to be int even if the stored struct field is u8; return-value promotion can be essential for matching.
Header and doxygen declarations should be updated together when fixing a function signature, as done for Camera_8002E6FC.
For callback-style game-mode functions, preserve the external signature even when the argument is unused.
State-machine matches often benefit from expressing exact frame thresholds and equality checks in source rather than hiding them behind named constants before the function is stable.
Struct signedness can affect generated loads and extensions; changing gm_8049E548_t.unk_8 from s8 to u8 suggests signedness was important to nearby matching behavior.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retain address-based names such as fn_80182F40, gm_801BF6D8, gm_801BF6F8, and Camera_8002E6FC.
Global labels retain lbl_ address-style names, e.g. lbl_804D65E8.
Register-influenced temporaries are named temp_r31 and temp_r31_2, matching common decomp scratch naming for register allocation-sensitive locals.
Unused callback parameters are explicitly named unused.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed small integer API types to int to avoid mismatched sign/zero extension or argument passing around Camera_8002E6FC and gm_801BF6D8/gm_801BF6F8.
Used separate local variables for the two focus-player selections in different switch cases, likely to match register allocation/lifetime.
Kept repeated calls to gm_801BF6D8 and gm_801BF6F8 in conditions and loops rather than caching everything, preserving likely original call structure.
Used explicit float literals 60.0F, 30.0F, and 0.017453292F in camera setup.
Structured the logic with early returns before the switch, matching a likely branch layout in the original assembly.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR context.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but the provided human_text_excerpt is empty, so no specific feedback can be attributed.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename fn_80182F40 or document its game-mode purpose.
Check whether Camera_8002E6FC has other call sites that also require int rather than u8.
Inspect gm_8049E548_t fields unk_8 and unk_9 for eventual semantic names tied to player slots or event participants.
Look for subsequent matches in gm_17C0.c around gm_80182DF0 and gm_80183218 that clarify the surrounding state machine.
